content:
Query: Create a runnable boids simulation in plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It should render fast-moving and rainbow-colored boids on a 2D canvas.

Requirements:
- The main runnable interface is `index.html`.
- Split the implementation into separate components and files.
- Verify that all files were created and wired together before returning done, with no syntax errors.


Use the REPL to inspect/decompose and act now. Choose the lane in code: `llm_query_batched(prompts)` for independent one-shot LLM prompts that do not need tools/files/iteration; `rlm_delegate(...)` plus `yield rlm_wait(*handles)` for independent units that need tools, files, execution, repair, verification, or multi-turn work. For multi-file or multi-component artifacts, spawn the child batch before writing unit outputs in root unless there is a hard sequential dependency. When delegating artifacts/components, keep each child `query` short and put the shared brief, owned scope, dependencies, interfaces, and acceptance checks in `context`; do not pass only the filename/unit name as context. Work directly only for one small local scope or truly sequential work. Do not call `done(...)` until the needed REPL work is complete.

Use exactly one fenced REPL code block per assistant message. Your entire reply must have this shape:
```repl
# Python code here
```
Do not write bare `repl` without the opening and closing triple backticks.

reply:
```repl
# Plan:
# 1. Spawn 4 child agents (index.html, css/styles.css, js/boids.js, js/main.js).
# 2. Each child receives the shared project brief + file-specific requirements and must write the file.
# 3. Wait for children to finish, then verify files exist, are wired together, and perform basic syntax checks (brace/paren matching, presence of expected tags/links).
# 4. If all checks pass, finish with done(message) listing created files and how to run.

shared_brief = (
    "Project: Fast-moving rainbow boids simulation on a 2D canvas.\n"
    "Requirements common to all files:\n"
    "- Keep code plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript (no build step).\n"
    "- Files to produce (paths relative to project root):\n"
    "  1) index.html\n"
    "  2) css/styles.css\n"
    "  3) js/boids.js\n"
    "  4) js/main.js\n"
    "- index.html must include a <canvas id=\"canvas\"></canvas>, link to css/styles.css, and include scripts for js/boids.js then js/main.js.\n"
    "- Boids must be rainbow-colored (hue cycles), fast-moving, and rendered on 2D canvas.\n"
    "- Keep code modular: boids.js defines and exports or exposes a Boid class and helper functions; main.js sets up canvas, creates many boids, animates, and uses boid functions.\n"
    "- Each child: write the assigned file exactly at the given path using write_file, and return only the path string when done.\n"
    "- Acceptance checks (parent will verify): files exist, index.html references css and scripts, basic JS brace/paren/bracket matching, and canvas element present.\n"
)

# File-specific contexts and short queries:
specs = [
    (
        "index_html",
        "Create index.html at path 'index.html' per the shared brief. Write valid HTML that links css/styles.css and includes js/boids.js then js/main.js. Return only the path when done.",
        shared_brief + "\n\nFile: index.html\nPath: index.html\nExtra notes:\n- Include a header title 'Rainbow Boids'.\n- Ensure script tags reference 'js/boids.js' then 'js/main.js' in that order.\n- Include a fullscreen canvas element with id 'canvas' and a small UI overlay (FPS counter optional).\n"
    ),
    (
        "css_styles",
        "Create CSS at 'css/styles.css' per the shared brief. Style the canvas to fill viewport and show black background; make boids bright. Return only the path when done.",
        shared_brief + "\n\nFile: css/styles.css\nPath: css/styles.css\nExtra notes:\n- Canvas should cover full window and have high-performance settings.\n- Add simple styles for body and FPS overlay.\n"
    ),
    (
        "js_boids",
        "Create js/boids.js per the shared brief. Implement a Boid class with position, velocity, update, draw(ctx, time) etc. Use hue-based coloring that cycles by time or velocity. Return only the path when done.",
        shared_brief + "\n\nFile: js/boids.js\nPath: js/boids.js\nExtra notes:\n- Provide an exported/global Boid constructor or class and helper functions: distance, limit, wrapPosition.\n- draw(ctx, time) should set stroke/fill style using HSL hue based on velocity angle or global time to make rainbow effect.\n- Keep code ES5/ES6 compatible for direct browser use; do not rely on module import syntax since index.html will load as plain scripts. Instead, attach Boid to window (window.Boid) to be used by main.js.\n"
    ),
    (
        "js_main",
        "Create js/main.js per the shared brief. Setup canvas, create many Boid instances, animate at high speed, and handle resize. Return only the path when done.",
        shared_brief + "\n\nFile: js/main.js\nPath: js/main.js\nExtra notes:\n- After DOM load, find canvas by id and set its size to innerWidth/innerHeight and handle resize.\n- Create ~150 boids with random positions and velocities (fast). Implement cohesion, separation, alignment at moderate strength to keep boids moving fast and flashy.\n- Use requestAnimationFrame for animation, pass time to draw for hue shifts.\n- Provide an FPS counter (optional) as an overlay element if present in HTML.\n"
    ),
]

# Spawn delegates
handles = [
    rlm_delegate(name=name, query=query, context=context)
    for name, query, context in specs
]

# Wait for children to finish
results = yield rlm_wait(*handles)

# Collect and print their returned messages
print("Child results (should be file paths):")
for r in results:
    print("-", repr(r))

# Verification steps:
# 1. Check files exist via ls
created_files = ls(".")
print("\nFiles in repo (partial listing):")
for p in created_files:
    print("-", p)

# We expect at least the four paths; create list of expected paths
expected_paths = ["index.html", os.path.join("css", "styles.css"), os.path.join("js", "boids.js"), os.path.join("js", "main.js")]
missing = [p for p in expected_paths if p not in created_files]

# Read files for further checks if present
file_contents = {}
for p in expected_paths:
    if p in created_files:
        file_contents[p] = read_file(p)
    else:
        file_contents[p] = None

# Basic checks:
issues = []

# 1) Missing files
if missing:
    issues.append("Missing files: " + ", ".join(missing))

# 2) index.html references
if file_contents["index.html"] is None:
    issues.append("index.html not found/readable.")
else:
    ih = file_contents["index.html"]
    if "<canvas" not in ih or "id=\"canvas\"" not in ih:
        issues.append("index.html does not contain a canvas with id=\"canvas\".")
    if "css/styles.css" not in ih:
        issues.append("index.html does not link to css/styles.css.")
    if "js/boids.js" not in ih or "js/main.js" not in ih:
        issues.append("index.html does not include script tags for js/boids.js and js/main.js in the right order.")
    if not ih.strip().lower().endswith("</html>"):
        # Not a strict failure but warn
        issues.append("index.html may be malformed or missing closing </html> tag.")

# 3) Basic JS brace/paren/bracket matching for both JS files
def simple_balance_check(s):
    # returns list of mismatch messages
    pairs = {'(':')','{':'}','[':']'}
    opens = []
    msgs = []
    for i,ch in enumerate(s):
        if ch in pairs:
            opens.append((ch,i))
        elif ch in pairs.values():
            if not opens:
                msgs.append(f"Unmatched closing {ch} at pos {i}")
            else:
                last, pos = opens[-1]
                if pairs[last] == ch:
                    opens.pop()
                else:
                    msgs.append(f"Mismatched {last} opened at {pos} but closed with {ch} at {i}")
                    opens.pop()
    for last,pos in opens:
        msgs.append(f"Unclosed {last} opened at {pos}")
    return msgs

for p in ["js/boids.js","js/main.js"]:
    content = file_contents.get(p)
    if content is None:
        issues.append(f"{p} not found for syntax check.")
    else:
        msgs = simple_balance_check(content)
        if msgs:
            issues.append(f"Syntax-like issues in {p}: " + "; ".join(msgs))

# 4) Check that boids.js defines Boid and main.js references it
if file_contents.get("js/boids.js") and "function Boid" not in file_contents["js/boids.js"] and "class Boid" not in file_contents["js/boids.js"]:
    if "window.Boid" not in file_contents["js/boids.js"]:
        issues.append("js/boids.js does not define a Boid constructor/class or attach window.Boid.")

if file_contents.get("js/main.js") and ("new Boid" not in file_contents["js/main.js"] and "Boid(" not in file_contents["js/main.js"]):
    issues.append("js/main.js does not appear to construct Boid instances (no 'new Boid' found).")

# 5) Check CSS existence/contents
if file_contents.get("css/styles.css") is None:
    issues.append("css/styles.css missing.")
else:
    css = file_contents["css/styles.css"]
    if "canvas" not in css and "body" not in css:
        issues.append("css/styles.css seems minimal; expected canvas/body rules.")

# Report issues or success. If issues, include file excerpts for debugging.
if issues:
    print("\nVerification issues detected:")
    for it in issues:
        print("-", it)
    # Provide small excerpts to help debugging
    for p, c in file_contents.items():
        if c:
            print(f"\n--- Start of {p} (first 400 chars) ---")
            print(c[:400])
            print(f"--- End excerpt ---")
    # Return a clear error via done with the issues (child outputs are available to inspect)
    done("VERIFICATION FAILED:\n" + "\n".join(issues))
else:
    # All good
    created_list = "\n".join(expected_paths)
    message = (
        "SUCCESS: All files created and basic wiring/syntax checks passed.\n\n"
        "Created files:\n" + created_list + "\n\n"
        "To run: open index.html in a modern browser (double-click or serve from a local static server).\n"
        "index.html includes a fullscreen canvas with rainbow fast-moving boids.\n"
    )
    done(message)
```

# 4) Check that boids.js defines Boid and main.js references it

        issues.append("js/boids.js does not define a Boid constructor/class or attach window.Boid.")

if file_contents.get("js/main.js") and ("new Boid" not in file_contents["js/main.js"] and "Boid(" not in file_contents["js/main.js"]):
    issues.append("js/main.js does not appear to construct Boid instances (no 'new Boid' found).")

# 5) Check CSS existence/contents
if file_contents.get("css/styles.css") is None:
    issues.append("css/styles.css missing.")
else:
    css = file_contents["css/styles.css"]
    if "canvas" not in css and "body" not in css:
        issues.append("css/styles.css seems minimal; expected canvas/body rules.")

# Report issues or success. If issues, include file excerpts for debugging.
if issues:
    print("\nVerification issues detected:")
    for it in issues:
        print("-", it)
    # Provide small excerpts to help debugging
    for p, c in file_contents.items():
        if c:
            print(f"\n--- Start of {p} (first 400 chars) ---")
            print(c[:400])
            print(f"--- End excerpt ---")
    # Return a clear error via done with the issues (child outputs are available to inspect)
    done("VERIFICATION FAILED:\n" + "\n".join(issues))
else:
    # All good
    created_list = "\n".join(expected_paths)
    message = (
        "SUCCESS: All files created and basic wiring/syntax checks passed.\n\n"
        "Created files:\n" + created_list + "\n\n"
        "To run: open index.html in a modern browser (double-click or serve from a local static server).\n"
        "index.html includes a fullscreen canvas with rainbow fast-moving boids.\n"
    )
    done(message)
